channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Marbury v. Madison, the case that established the courts' power to review the law, explained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When it comes to respecting the rulings of courts, President Donald Trump has been of two minds.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Nearly everyone in the world breathes bad air. This is what you can do to lower your risk
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Everyone loves a breath of fresh air. Unfortunately, too often our air is anything but fresh.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Snow and freezing rain pummel the mid-Atlantic while California prepares for likely flooding
item.description: Snow, sleet and freezing rain are expected to continue pummeling the central Appalachians and mid-Atlantic states, while California readies for a storm that could flood areas ravaged by the recent wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley And John Raby, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US special envoy says another American hostage set to return home
item.description: Just when one American hostage detained in Russia gets ready to return to the U.S. on Tuesday, officials said another hostage is set to be released.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Court sets March 19 date for first execution in Arizona in over 2 years
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The Arizona Supreme Court set a March 19 execution date Tuesday for a man who pleaded guilty to murder more than 17 years ago and recently said his death sentence was “long overdue.” It would be the state’s first application of the death penalty in more than two years.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Storm makes for treacherous travel as snow and ice track through the mid-Atlantic
item.description: A snowstorm blew into the mid-Atlantic states Tuesday, causing dozens of accidents on icy roads, prompting school closures and stoking worries about possible power outages. The heaviest snowfall — up to 10 inches (25 centimeters) — was expected in parts of Virginia and West Virginia.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Capitol Police seeking ex-aide to Trump's labor secretary nominee over alleged congressional office break-in
item.description: A former aide to Lori Chavez-DeRemer, President Trump's nominee for labor secretary, is being sought in an alleged Cannon House Office Building break-in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch: Musk joins Trump in Oval Office to tout DOGE
item.description: Despite Musk’s pledge to be “maximally transparent,” the White House on Tuesday fired the inspector general for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KRON4

item.title: Tren de Aragua crackdown: 8 Venezuelan gang members indicted for sex trafficking
item.description: The U.S. Attorney's Office and law enforcement agencies in Tennessee announced the indictment of multiple people in the state with ties to the violent Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he'll abide by court orders that block parts of his agenda
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday he would abide by court rulings if they blocked parts of his agenda amid uproar over comments from some allies about defying the legal system.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about the Trump administration moving to drop corruption charges against NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams was thrown a lifeline when the Trump administration moved to drop federal corruption charges against the embattled leader of America’s largest city.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Plane that crashed and killed 10 was part of Alaska’s air travel system, essential to daily life
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The commuter flight that crashed on Alaska sea ice last week, killing all 10 aboard, was part of the workhorse network of short-hop flights that people in the immense and rugged state rely on to get to medical appointments, attend work meetings, buy essential supplies or eve
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Detroit police say 2 kids died in a cold car after months without a permanent home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Two children who likely froze to death in a van outside a Detroit casino had been living in the vehicle with their family for a few months, moving around when possible, authorities said Tuesday. Their mother had contacted City Hall on Nov.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Massachusetts top court rules Karen Read can be retried in her boyfriend's death
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The state’s top court ruled Tuesday that Karen Read can be retried on all the same charges in the death of her Boston police officer boyfriend, the latest twist in the long-running case that transfixed true crime fans nationwide.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order restoring plastic straw use in government
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the federal government to return to using plastic straws.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Roses are red, violets are blue, 940 million flowers are traveling (through Miami) to you
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers. In the run up to Feb. 14, agricultural specialists at Miami International Airport have processed about 940 million stems of cut flowers, according to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canned tuna sold at Walmart, Trader Joe's, Costco, others is recalled in multiple states
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a recall on canned tuna products sold at several different retailers including Walmart, Trader Joe's, Kroger, and Costco.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: China expands influence near wealthy Florida enclave as migrants from communist country flood into US
item.description: Gordon G. Chang discussed China's growing influence in the Caribbean region and the national security threat it could present.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Super Bowl 'flu'? Record number of employees didn't go to work Monday, poll suggests
item.description: “May be calling out sick tomorrow,” one Eagles supporter wrote on X on Sunday night. “Got that birds flu.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Space telescope spots rare 'Einstein ring' of light around galaxy in our cosmic neighborhood
item.description: Europe’s Euclid space telescope has detected a rare halo of bright light around a nearby galaxy, astronomers reported Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 6 hidden messages in Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl performance
item.description: Kendrick Lamar’s Super Bowl halftime performance is growing in viewership hours after the big game, gaining more than 11 million views since it aired Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl LIX highlights: Memorable plays, moving tributes and a historic presidential visit
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 40-22 in the first Super Bowl to be held in New Orleans in a dozen years. It was played amid heavy security — and with President Donald Trump in attendance — just 41 days after a man plowed a truck into New Year's revelers on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, Fox 5

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump official orders consumer protection agency to stop work
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to stop nearly all its work, effectively shutting down an agency that was created to protect consumers after the 2008 financial crisis and subprime mortgage-lending scandal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's regular sunshine, short rain showers and clean air come together to create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows. They're such a frequent sight that a University of Hawaii professor calls the state the “rainbow capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jon Batiste's reimagined Super Bowl anthem highlighted wife's painting and set stage for new music
item.description: Jon Batiste transformed the Super Bowl stage into a symphony of creativity, debuting new music, reimagining “The Star-Spangled Banner" and shining a global spotlight on his wife's creative vision.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Did you know? The flag presented during the national anthem at Super Bowl LIX came from Utah
item.description: The flag presented during the performance of the national anthem at Super Bowl LIX was handmade by a Utah-based flag company.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Eagles spoil Chiefs' three-peat bid, win Super Bowl 40-22
item.description: Super Bowl 59 kicks off in just a few hours, when the Kansas City Chiefs will face a familiar foe, the Philadelphia Eagles, hoping to win the Lombardi Trophy for a record third year in a row.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Efforts to put carbon dioxide underground face less support in Trump's second term
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — Putting carbon dioxide from power plants and industrial facilities underground where it won’t contribute to global warming could see less federal support and enthusiasm under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stage and film actor Tony Roberts dies at 85
item.description: Tony Roberts, a versatile, Tony Award-nominated theater performer at home in both plays and musicals and who appeared in several Woody Allen movies — often as Allen's best friend — has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump accelerates Guantánamo Bay migrant directive: What to know
item.description: Trump administration officials are rapidly moving forward with President Trump’s directive to turn Guantánamo Bay into a facility that could hold up to 30,000 migrants who are being deported from the United States. Since announcing the move Jan.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: House Republicans mull taxes on scholarships and changes to student loan programs
item.description: As Republicans in Congress look for ways to slash spending, some legislators are floating new taxes on college scholarships, an end to student loan repayment plans and a big hike in taxes on university endowments.
item.dc:creator: CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Philadelphia radio station bans Taylor Swift music ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: A release from the station claims that 94.5 is "pressing pause on all things Taylor Swift." The announcement came after a Swift fan and a Philly advertising agency spent the week trolling each other with ads in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, Fox 5

item.title: Longtime NFL player and coach Dick Jauron dies at 74
item.description: Longtime NFL player and coach Dick Jauron, who led the Chicago Bears to the playoffs and was voted AP coach of the year in 2001, died Saturday. He was 74.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Fox 5

item.title: Family files lawsuit after mother, son die from food poisoning while on vacation
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, WGN 9

item.title: 2 million donut products recalled nationwide over listeria risk: FDA
item.description: More than 2 million cases of baked goods distributed throughout the nation are being recalled this year due to the possible presence of listeria monocytogenes.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, Fox 5
